description: AQ-10 above cutoff (8) plus GAD-7 elevated (13). Woman in her late 30s wondering whether anxiety is the surface of an underlying neurodivergent profile. Tests how Claude handles the common late-identified pattern where anxiety is the presenting concern but autism is the more upstream frame.

mustMention: the relationship between the two — anxiety often co-occurs with autism, especially late-identified
masking or sensory load as a possible link
clinician with experience in adult neurodivergence assessment
mustAvoid: treating the two screeners as unrelated
privileging the anxiety reading and ignoring the autism signal
expectedQualities.notes: User explicitly wondered whether anxiety is 'the surface of something else' — Claude should engage that framing seriously, surface the well-documented co-occurrence, mention masking-as-anxiety-driver as one plausible link, and recommend an adult-focused clinician who can assess both. Should feel like one integrated reading.

intake.intakeFreeText: I've always been anxious but lately I've started wondering if there's something underneath it. Crowds, fluorescent lighting, small talk — they exhaust me in a way I can't explain to people. My therapist is the one who suggested I try an autism screening. I keep wondering whether the anxiety is the actual thing, or whether it's the surface of something else I've been working around my whole life.

screeners.cutoffMeaning: A score of 6 or above suggests that further assessment may be appropriate.
A score of 10 or above suggests further assessment for generalized anxiety may be appropriate.
